default_fridge: {brand} fridge installs usually get tight at the top panel and rear wall. This page brings the working clearance numbers together and points you to the {count} models we track for this range.
default_washing_machine: {brand} washing machine installs are easier to shortlist when cabinet space and service room are checked together. Use these figures as the first pass before you compare exact models.
default_dishwasher: {brand} dishwasher fit checks usually come down to side room, service depth and how tidy the cavity opening really is. Start with these figures, then move to the model list.
default_dryer: {brand} dryer installs look simple until the wall gap, venting plan and door access are counted properly. This page gives you the clearance numbers first so the shortlist stays realistic.
samsung_fridge: Samsung fridge installs often hinge on the top gap, especially below overhead cabinetry. Start with the clearances here, then check height and depth on the models that still look promising.
lg_fridge: LG fridge cabinets often leave more usable height than shoppers expect once the top gap is counted correctly. Use this page to set the clearances first, then compare the models that suit your kitchen.
fisher-paykel_fridge: Fisher & Paykel fridges are often chosen late in a renovation when the joinery is already fixed. This page lets you check the working clearances before you spend time on individual model specs.
westinghouse_fridge: Westinghouse fridge ranges cover narrow cabinets and very wide family units, so the airflow gaps matter early. Use this page to lock the clearances in before you shortlist by dimensions.
hisense_fridge: Hisense fridges cover a broad spread of widths, which makes early filtering worthwhile. Check the clearances here first, then compare the models that still fit the opening you have.
chiq_fridge: CHiQ fridges can look compact on paper, but the cabinet still needs room to breathe. Start with the clearance numbers here, then narrow the list to the models that suit your space.
